mission.title: oras: Bad UX of `--oci-layout-path` flag working with `oras repo tags`
mission.description: Bad UX of `--oci-layout-path` flag working with `oras repo tags`. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current oras deployment
steps.description:
Verify your oras version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras
helm list -n oras 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working oras installation.

steps.title: Review oras configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant oras configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras
kubectl get configmap -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=oras
```
### What happened in your environment?

I have an OCI layout that contains several tags, some are in format of FQDN.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Bad UX of `--oci-layout-path` flag working with `oras repo…
steps.description:
This PR implements the new feature of showing repo tags with `--oci-layout-path` in `repo tags` command. This feature applies to the scenario where the user stores images from multiple repositories in the same OCI layout directory, and put the full references of the images as tags. For example, an
```yaml
$ oras repo tags --oci-layout ./repo
example.registry.com/foo:latest
test.com/bar:v1
v2
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras
kubectl get events -n oras --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Bad UX of `--oci-layout-path` flag working with `oras repo…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This PR implements the new feature of showing repo tags with `--oci-layout-path` in `repo tags` command. This feature applies to the scenario where the user stores images from multiple repositories in the same OCI layout directory, and put the full references of the images as tags.
codeSnippets:
$ oras repo tags --oci-layout ./repo
example.registry.com/foo:latest
test.com/bar:v1
v2

$ oras repo tags --oci-layout-path ./repo
Error: "oras repo tags" requires exactly 1 argument but got 0
Usage: oras repo tags [flags] <name>
Please specify exactly 1 argument as the target repository to list tags from. Run "oras repo tags -h" for more options and examples

oras repo tags --oci-layout-path ./repo ""
example.registry.com/foo:latest
test.com/bar:v1
v2

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with oras installed or the issue environment reproducible.
